infobox.key: Nora Leticia Rocha de la Cruz
infobox.value: (born 18 December 1967) is a retired female [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") [athlete](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") from [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), who competed in the [5000](/wiki/5000_metres "5000 metres") and [10,000 metres](/wiki/10,000_metres "10,000 metres"). She claimed the [gold medal](/wiki/Gold_medal "Gold medal") in the women's 10,000 metres at the [1999 Pan American Games](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1999_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 1999 Pan American Games") in [Winnipeg](/wiki/Winnipeg "Winnipeg"), Manitoba, Canada.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican long-distance runner

Nora Rocha

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1967-12-18) 18 December 1967 (age 58)  [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [Athletics](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") | | | | Representing  [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") | | | | [Pan American Games](/wiki/Pan_American_Games "Pan American Games") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [1999 Winnipeg](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1999_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 1999 Pan American Games") | 10,000 metres | | Silver medal – second place | [2003 Santo Domingo](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2003_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 2003 Pan American Games") | [5,000 metres](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2003_Pan_American_Games_-_Women%27s_5000_metres "Athletics at the 2003 Pan American Games - Women's 5000 metres") | | Bronze medal – third place | [2007 Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2007_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 2007 Pan American Games") | [5,000 metres](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2007_Pan_American_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_5000_metres "Athletics at the 2007 Pan American Games – Women's 5000 metres") | | [Central American and Caribbean Games](/wiki/Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Central American and Caribbean Games") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [1998 Maracaibo](/wiki/1998_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "1998 Central American and Caribbean Games") | [5,000 metres](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1998_Central_American_and_Caribbean_Games "Athletics at the 1998 Central American and Caribbean Games") | | |

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Rocha* and the second or maternal family name is  *de la Cruz*.

**Nora Leticia Rocha de la Cruz** (born 18 December 1967) is a retired female [track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") [athlete](/wiki/Athletics_(sport) "Athletics (sport)") from [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"), who competed in the [5000](/wiki/5000_metres "5000 metres") and [10,000 metres](/wiki/10,000_metres "10,000 metres"). She claimed the [gold medal](/wiki/Gold_medal "Gold medal") in the women's 10,000 metres at the [1999 Pan American Games](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1999_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 1999 Pan American Games") in [Winnipeg](/wiki/Winnipeg "Winnipeg"), Manitoba, Canada.
